en: He who is often reproved, yet stiffens his neck, will suddenly be broken beyond healing.
zh: 人屢次受責備，仍然硬著頸項， 他必突然毀滅，無法挽救。

en: When the righteous increase, the people rejoice, but when the wicked rule, the people groan.
zh: 義人增多的時候，人民就喜樂； 惡人掌權的時候，人民就歎息。

en: He who loves wisdom makes his father glad, but a companion of prostitutes squanders his wealth.
zh: 喜愛智慧的，使父親喜樂； 親近妓女的，耗盡家財。

en: By justice a king builds up the land, but he who exacts gifts tears it down.
zh: 君王以公正使國堅立； 收受賄賂的，使國覆亡。

en: A man who flatters his neighbor spreads a net for his feet.
zh: 諂媚鄰舍的人， 是在他的腳下張設網羅。

en: An evil man is ensnared in his transgression, but a righteous man sings and rejoices.
zh: 惡人因為過犯，陷於網羅； 義人卻歡呼喜樂。

en: A righteous man knows the rights of the poor; a wicked man does not understand such knowledge.
zh: 義人關注窮人的冤情； 惡人卻不分辨實情。

en: Scoffers set a city aflame, but the wise turn away wrath.
zh: 好譏笑人的煽動全城騷亂， 智慧人卻止息眾怒。

en: If a wise man has an argument with a fool, the fool only rages and laughs, and there is no quiet.
zh: 智慧人與愚妄人爭訟， 愚妄人只會咆哮或嘲笑，總不能安靜。

en: Bloodthirsty men hate one who is blameless and seek the life of the upright.
zh: 好流人血的恨惡完全人， 並尋索正直人的性命。

en: A fool gives full vent to his spirit, but a wise man quietly holds it back.
zh: 愚昧人把怒氣盡情發洩， 智慧人卻抑制怒氣。

en: If a ruler listens to falsehood, all his officials will be wicked.
zh: 如果掌權者聽信謊言， 他所有的臣僕必都是壞人。

en: The poor man and the oppressor meet together; the LORD gives light to the eyes of both.
zh: 世上有窮人，也有欺壓人的， 兩者的眼睛都蒙耶和華光照。

en: If a king faithfully judges the poor, his throne will be established forever.
zh: 如果君王誠實地審判窮人； 他的國位必永遠堅立。

en: The rod and reproof give wisdom, but a child left to himself brings shame to his mother.
zh: 杖責和管教能使人有智慧， 放縱的孩子使母親蒙羞。

en: When the wicked increase, transgression increases, but the righteous will look upon their downfall.
zh: 惡人增多的時候，過犯也必增多； 義人必看見他們傾覆。

en: Discipline your son, and he will give you rest; he will give delight to your heart.
zh: 管教你的兒子，他必使你得安息； 也必使你的心得喜樂。

en: Where there is no prophetic vision the people cast off restraint, but blessed is he who keeps the law.
zh: 沒有啟示，人民就沒有法紀； 遵守律法的，就為有福。

en: By mere words a servant is not disciplined, for though he understands, he will not respond.
zh: 只用言語，不能使奴僕受管教； 他雖然明白，卻沒有反應。

en: Do you see a man who is hasty in his words? There is more hope for a fool than for him.
zh: 你見過言語急躁的人嗎？ 愚昧人比他更有指望。

en: Whoever pampers his servant from childhood will in the end find him his heir.
zh: 如果人從小嬌縱僕人， 結果必帶來憂愁。

en: A man of wrath stirs up strife, and one given to anger causes much transgression.
zh: 容易發怒的人，引起紛爭； 脾氣暴烈的人，多有過犯。

en: One's pride will bring him low, but he who is lowly in spirit will obtain honor.
zh: 人的驕傲必使他卑微； 心裡謙卑的，必得尊榮。

en: The partner of a thief hates his own life; he hears the curse, but discloses nothing.
zh: 與盜賊分贓的，是恨惡自己的性命； 他雖然聽見要他起誓作證的聲音， 卻不說話。

en: The fear of man lays a snare, but whoever trusts in the LORD is safe.
zh: 懼怕人的，必陷入網羅； 倚靠耶和華的，必得安全。

en: Many seek the face of a ruler, but it is from the LORD that a man gets justice.
zh: 很多人求掌權者的情面， 但各人的判決是出於耶和華。

en: An unjust man is an abomination to the righteous, but one whose way is straight is an abomination to the wicked.
zh: 不義的人是義人所厭惡的； 行為正直的人是惡人所厭惡的。
